REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JUSTIN TIN, SIU CHAN DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO STATE THEIR CAUSES OF ACTION IN CLEAR AND CONCISE LANGUAGE; CLEARLY LABEL WHICH DEFENDANTS SPECIFIC CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BEING ASSERTED AS TO; AND ALLEGE FACTS AND CAUSES OF ACTION THAT ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF ACTION CGC-22-558372 AND FILINGS THEREIN IS GRANTED. THE COURT DOES NOT MAKE A RULING ON THE COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL/RES JUDICATA ARGUMENT AT THIS TIME. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER WAS NON-RESPONSIVE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS UNDER CCP 128.5 IS DENIED DUE TO FAILURE TO SHOW COMPLIANCE WITH PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS OF THE STATUTE, E.G. SEPARATE MOTION, SAFE HARBOR PROVISION, ETC. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT JUSTIN TIN, SIU CHAN DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to state their causes of action in clear and concise language; clearly label which defendants specific causes of action are being asserted as to; and allege facts and causes of action that are not barred by the statute of limitations. Defendant's request for judicial notice of action CGC-22-558372 and filings therein is granted. The Court does not make a ruling on the collateral estoppel/res judicata argument at this time. The Court notes that Plaintiff's opposition to the demurrer was non-responsive. Defendant's request for sanctions under CCP 128.5 is denied due to failure to show compliance with procedural requirements of the statute, e.g. separate motion, safe harbor provision, etc.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Mar-13-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT HOA COOC NOTICE OF MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show any new or different facts not presented or not capable of having been presented to the Court on or before January 30, 2024. The Court notes that this motion is not supported by Points and Authorities.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Jan-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:

Hoa Cooc Esq., Law Offices of Hoa T. Cooc, 510-268-8063, in-person for himself
No other appearances.

Mr. Cooc contests the tentative. Mr. Cooc states that he did not give notice that he will be contesting. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HOA COOC NOTICE OF MOTION & MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT is DENIED. Moving party failed to show surprise, mistake or excusable neglect. Additionally, the motion is not accompanied by a proposed response.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
